mission.title: keycloak: External IDP tokens are not refreshed automatically for OAuth2 & OIDC IDPs when retrieving the external token
mission.description: External IDP tokens are not refreshed automatically for OAuth2 & OIDC IDPs when retrieving the external token. Requested by 62+ users.

steps.title: Check current keycloak deployment
steps.description:
Verify your keycloak version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n keycloak -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keycloak
helm list -n keycloak 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working keycloak installation.

steps.title: Review keycloak configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant keycloak configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n keycloak -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keycloak
kubectl get configmap -n keycloak -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=keycloak
```
### Describe the bug

We're using Github (specifically a Github app) as an external IDP. We have configured Github to use expiring access tokens (good for 8 hours).

steps.title: Apply the fix for External IDP tokens are not refreshed automatically for
steps.description:
I think, this is only explaining that using the refresh token outside of Keycloak is no viable workaround.
"If the refresh token is still good, it is worthless because using it will expire the one held in Keycloak." 

When Keycloak refreshes the access token in time, the app will never need the refresh token as assumed by .

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n keycloak -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keycloak
kubectl get events -n keycloak --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "External IDP tokens are not refreshed automatically for" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
I think, this is only explaining that using the refresh token outside of Keycloak is no viable workaround.
"If the refresh token is still good, it is worthless because using it will expire the one held in Keycloak." 

When Keycloak refreshes the access token in time, the app will never need the refresh token as assumed by .

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with keycloak installed or the issue environment reproducible.
